value.Title: STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-3F43-SHSP-W0MN-00000-00STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSTOCKSTONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC

PermID4297094217Websitehttps://www.stonepoint.com/private-equity/IndustryPrivate EquityAddress20 Horseneck Lane Greenwich, CT 06830

ACTIVITIES:

Stone Point Capital LLC, founded in 2005, is a Greenwich, Connecticut-based private equity firm managing $45 billion in assets. Specializing in financial services, it invests in insurance, asset management, and fintech companies. With 70 professionals, Stone Point targets investments between $50 million and $1 billion. Notable portfolio companies include Alliant Insurance and Focus Financial Partners. The firms industry expertise and operational support drive portfolio growth. Stone Points disciplined investment strategy and strong industry relationships ensure consistent value creation.

+ STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC [1 News That Matters]

+ VENSURE NETS $450 MILLION DELAYED DRAW FACILITY TO DRIVE GROWTH AND M&A

Vensure Employer Solutions has secured $450 million from Stone Point Capital Markets to back its M&A strategy, via a Delayed Draw Term Loan upsized from $300 million. The financing was announced on the 23rd of March 2026.The funds will support continued growth and more acquisitions, with CEO Alex Campos acknowledging Stone Point's ongoing support.Since 2025, Vensure has integrated over 100 companies, including 76 post-2021 deals. The company serves more than 161,000 clients and processes more than $150 billion in payroll annually.Recent technology acquisitions, such as Distro and CreAI, broaden Vensure's automation and data intelligence capabilities, with further transactions anticipated this year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) EXTENDS SENIOR NOTES FINANCING WITH $508 MILLION FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through a private offering of senior unsecured notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1) as of 11th May 2026. Proceeds will fund acquisitions or investments in new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States.CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, RNG, and transportation infrastructure projects. The platform started with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and December 2025 added $500 million. This issue marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million issuance in June 2025, expanding total investment capacity toward $5 billion. Velasco of KKR cited strong investor reception and noted the asset base’s quality and diversity underpin the platform’s ability to deploy more than $4 billion of investment capacity. McMahon of HASI highlighted the expansion of CCH1’s investment capacity to meet growing sustainable-infrastructure activity. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities served as Joint Lead Placement Agents.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TRANSFERS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) transfers its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move disclosed on May 11, 2026 at 21:07:57.692343. The sale aligns with KKR's plan to optimize holdings and concentrate on core investments within the real estate sector. The transaction underscores KKR's disciplined asset-management approach amid global economic uncertainty and reinforces its emphasis on sustainability as reflected in its MSCI ESG score. Stakeholders will monitor potential effects on future strategy and overall portfolio performance.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) DIVESTS DANISH RESIDENTIAL ASSETS AS PEARS GLOBAL EXPANDS NORTHERN EUROPEAN FOOTPRINT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) has sold its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, with the transaction announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343.The portfolio consists of several residential units located in key urban areas, and Pears Global Real Estate intends to expand its Northern European footprint.KKR frames the sale as part of its asset-management optimization, reallocating capital toward higher-return opportunities, including private credit.Pears Global Real Estate will apply its regional expertise to enhance the properties and support its European expansion amid rising housing demand.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) EXPAND PARTNERSHIP TO SCALE PRIVATE MARKET OFFERINGS FOR RETIREMENT PLANS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) expanded their partnership to provide private market investment solutions for wealth and retirement investors, as demand for alternatives grows across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the collaboration aims to broaden access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution offerings. The initiative builds on a long-standing relationship centered on fund administration and technology services, pairing Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing to offer tailored solutions for navigating private market investments. Michael Lane of SEI emphasized the need for simpler access to private markets, while Carlyle’s Jeff Nedelman highlighted the rising role of private markets in wealth and retirement planning. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported roughly $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. The partners expect the expanded collaboration to enhance the delivery of private market strategies to a broader investor base.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ADVANCES REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT THROUGH INTEGRATED HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is combining Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The integrated platform targets physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers, aiming to address administrative complexity and evolving care models. Knack supplies a globally distributed operational framework while Equalize adds predictive analytics and process optimization tools to improve revenue cycle management, billing, and payer coordination. The move reflects a broader trend toward technology-driven healthcare services as automation and data-driven systems become central to clinical administration.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD INTEGRATED RCM PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, broadening its healthcare portfolio. The May 4, 2026 release notes the transaction includes a majority stake in both firms, with terms undisclosed. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the platform to support ongoing developmentKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership aims to address rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views this as a step to deepen its presence in the RCM sector and to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementThe deal underscores Carlyle's focus on technology-enabled revenue cycle management and the potential to deliver enhanced, analytics-driven solutions to providers

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-95G3-SFDD-V19G-00000-00Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Renewable energy investor Skyview Ventures and its solar firm Davis Hill Development has expanded its partnership with HiveTracks to integrate apiaries and environmental monitoring across its solar portfolio.

The companys current solar offerings span multiple states, including Maryland, Connecticut, and New York, plus Washington, D.C. Using HiveTracks biodiversity intelligence platform will allow the company to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across several solar projects, incorporating pollinator habitats into existing solar projects.

This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind, says Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems.

Skyview and HiveTracks have been working together since 2025, the companies say, beginning their collaboration with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Thanks to the partnerships initial success, the two firms have planned expansion to additional Davis Hill sites throughout 2026.

Local beekeepers will tend to each solar site throughout the lifespan of its given apiary, the company says. Beyond the production of bottles of honey which in 2025 was given away to food banks the apiaries will serve as environmental indicators, giving the companies numbers on ecosystem health and land use impact over time.

solar apiary

Apiary analysis

As part of the agreement, HiveTrack will collect environmental metrics for the solar sites, both on a per-site and portfolio-wide level, company officials say. The metrics will give insight to pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, as well as testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology in each hive.

Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further, says Maria Morales Ferrebus, solar development associate at Skyview Ventures. In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk.

By implementing HiveTracks recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats.

The apiary program currently spans multiple projects throughout varying stages of development, the company says, with some affected projects already fully operational. This approach allows Skyview to monitor environmental impacts of solar projects through each stage of development, representatives say, with HiveTracks providing site-specific recommendations.

The heart of the program reflects a wider trend around the solar industry, Skyview says, where developers now have an outsized focus on multi-use land strategies. As part of the shift, solar projects have become synonymous with farming, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives around the U.S. and beyond.

Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale, says Max Rnzel, CEO of HiveTracks. Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact.

value.Title: OAK Global and New Energy Risk Announce Lloyd 's Coverholder Arrangement for Tax Credit Insurance Partnership supports financing of renewable energy and low-carbon infrastructure in the U.S.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-T3J3-RYWR-22WT-00000-00OAK Global and New Energy Risk Announce Lloyd's Coverholder Arrangement for Tax Credit Insurance2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisOAK Global and New Energy Risk Announce Lloyd's Coverholder Arrangement for Tax Credit InsurancePartnership supports financing of renewable energy and low-carbon infrastructure in the U.S.AVON, Conn. & LONDON

New Energy Risk ('NER'), a specialist in technology performance insurance with more than a decade of experience underwriting complex technology projects, and OAK Global , a leading risk partner that combines science and data-driven analytics with extensive underwriting expertise, today announced that NER has obtained Lloyd's coverholder status for its tax credit insurance business. OAK Global has sponsored NER's application and will underwrite the business through Syndicate 2843 (OAK Reinsurance) as part of its Transition TCX class, extending OAK Global's U.S. presence in the climate and technology space.

The coverholder appointment, granted via NER's parent company, Paragon Insurance Group on 1 April 2026, authorises NER to underwrite and bind insurance on behalf of Lloyd's syndicates. It extends the capacity behind NER's tax credit insurance products, which address the availability, transferability and monetisation of tax credits, and which play a practical role in the financing of clean energy projects in the U.S.

"Achieving Lloyd's coverholder status represents an important validation of our underwriting discipline, risk management framework, and expertise in tax credit risk," said George Schulz , CEO of NER. "Our ability to combine technical underwriting, legal expertise, actuarial rigor and market insight allows us to provide scalable, high-quality insurance solutions that help investors, developers and lenders manage tax credit risks with confidence."

"We are excited to work with OAK Global as a leading capacity partner," added Mr Schulz. "Their innovative underwriting philosophy, combined with deep underwriting expertise and focus on execution, is an opportunity that will allow us to grow this market."

For OAK Global, the sponsorship sits within OAK Horizon, a strategic business unit which commenced operations on 1 January 2026 and is focused on underwriting across the climate and technology ecosystem.

"New Energy Risk has built significant expertise in a segment that's critical to the U.S. energy transition," said Cathal Carr , Founder and CEO of OAK Global. "We're pleased to sponsor their coverholder status at Lloyd's and to work alongside them through OAK Horizon, where tax credit insurance fits naturally with other innovative structures supporting the financing of low-carbon infrastructure."

About New Energy Risk:

New Energy Risk is a pioneer of large-scale, breakthrough technology performance insurance solutions. The company provides complex risk assessment and serves as a bridge between technology innovators, financiers, and insurers. Insurance policies are administered through New Energy Risk affiliate Complex Risk and Insurance Associates, LLC, CA License #0I24307. Learn more: newenergyrisk.com .

About Paragon:

Paragon Insurance Holdings, LLC, formed in 2014, writes all commercial lines of insurance across more than 20 programs. Paragon's industry-specific and general underwriting facilities offer insureds, retail agents, carriers, reinsurers and service providers unique product, service, capability, and results. Learn more: paragoninsgroup.com .

About OAK Global

OAK Global, OAK Reinsurance, OAK Enterprise and OAK Horizon are trading names of OAK Servco Limited (company number 15702736). OAK Servco Limited is an Appointed Representative of Polo Managing Agency Limited (company registration number 03935227, FCA firm reference number 204976). In an era of evolving risk, we support our clients today in building resilience for tomorrow. Combining science and data-driven analytics with extensive underwriting expertise, we deliver innovative risk solutions and leading insight. Through our commitment to collaborative, long-term partnerships, our team tailors these solutions to each client's specific needs.

Underwriting through Syndicates 2843 and 1440, rooted in the strength of Lloyd's which holds Financial Strength ratings of: A+ AM Best, AA- S&P and AA- Fitch.

Find out more here: www.oakglobal.com

value.Title: General Assembly Passes Solar, Labor Bills As Session Winds Down

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGS-W5T3-RRYN-M2C6-00000-00General Assembly Passes Solar, Labor Bills As Session Winds Down2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:42ZLexisNexisSTATE CAPITOLGeneral Assembly Passes Solar, Labor Bills As Session Winds DownJamil Ragland

HARTFORD, CT – The Connecticut General Assembly advanced major bills on solar energy and labor-related issues Friday evening as the legislative session rushes toward its conclusion Wednesday.

The House spent more than three hours debating House Bill 5340, which would extend the state's Residential Renewable Energy Solutions program that levies renewable energy tariffs to compensate residential solar owners for the power their systems produce and provide to the local electric grid.

Rep. Tracy Marra, a Republican from Darien and ranking member on the Energy and Technology Committee, said that there was a lack of clarity regarding the ultimate impact the bill will have on ratepayers, and drew attention to the controversial public benefits section of electricity bills.

"Clearly this is going to be something that affects the bills of everyone across the state of Connecticut," Marra said. "When we have been hearing from customers everywhere, please lower our rates, please lower our rates. This is not what we contemplated here. We're contemplating putting more on."

However, Republicans were not uniform in opposition to the bill. Rep. Tim Ackert, R-Coventry, noted that there are 2,400 jobs based in the solar industry in the state, and called the bill's focus on battery storage and other renewables an investment.

"We need local power here in Connecticut," he said. "We're doing that with the last section of this bill, with our fuel cells. We're doing that with solar, and we have localized solar generation right here in Connecticut. We're not relying on Maine, or a power supply coming all the way from Canada here on power lines. So I look at this a little bit differently."

The bill passed the House 99-43, with 9 absent or not voting and is now on the Senate calendar for consideration. The Senate held a session this Saturday, but it was unclear if the bill would come up for consideration.

Connor Yakaitis, Deputy Director of the Connecticut League of Conservation Voters, celebrated the House vote.

"The Rate Payer Impact statement speaks for itself," Yakaitis said in an emailed statement. "These programs reduce rates to electric customers. Promoting solar energy means championing affordability in Connecticut. We look forward to the Senate considering this important legislation."

Senate Approves Omnibus Labor Bill

Meanwhile, the Senate spent most of Friday debating HB 5003, a sweeping bill that consolidates several bills the Labor and Public Employees committee considered during the session. It passed the House on April 28.

Major provisions of the bill include job protections for healthcare workers and teachers, enhancements to benefits for public safety officers and firefighters to improve recruitment, requirements for employers to share wage range information in job postings, and more.

The Connecticut Education Association (CEA) celebrated the bill's passage out of the Senate with a statement saying teachers across the state "are breathing a collective sigh of relief, no longer fearing that arbitrary decisions could force them out of the profession they love."

The bill ensures that disciplinary and termination decisions related to teachers will be "made through a fair, consistent, and objective process based on facts, not politics, pressure, or personal bias," according to CEA. "It is especially fitting that this bill advanced on May Day, International Workers' Day, when we honor the labor movement's continuing fight for fair treatment on the job."

CEA President Kate Dias expressed gratitude after the vote.

"We are extremely grateful to all the legislators who voted for this bill as well as to legislative leadership and Governor Lamont for championing educators," she said. "When this bill becomes law, educators will have fair and consistent treatment, just like other professionals. When decisions that affect an educator's career are handled through a fair and transparent process, schools are more stable, experienced educators remain in the profession, and students benefit."

Teachers facing discipline or termination are currently entitled to a hearing before a neutral hearing officer, but the officer's decision is only a recommendation to local school boards in Connecticut. Unlike with other school employees, the local school board makes the final decision.

CEA said the current process is "politically biased" and "creates a climate of fear that impacts the entire teaching environment."

Dias continued: "Every teacher deserves to be treated with fairness and respect, and this bill ensures teachers receive a fair, consistent, objective, and professional process based on facts and evidence regarding disciplinary and termination decisions. This legislation is critical to ensuring that decisions affecting an educator's career and reputation are handled with integrity, strengthening trust in our schools and allowing our communities to keep great educators in the classroom doing what they do best, teach."

Sen. Rob Sampson, a Republican from Wolcott and ranking member on the labor committee, took issue with the process for the bill, calling the omnibus package a "legislative buffet" that moved beyond the boundaries of labor issues.

"How did we get here?" he asked. "Why do we have a 75 [section] bill that is a container, effectively, for many, many bills that ran as individual bills themselves and now just end up as a section or two or three throughout this huge document? And how did we get to a point where we're including lobsters and double utility poles in a labor bill?"

After six hours of debate and multiple failed amendments, the bill passed the Senate 28-7 with one absent or not voting. The bill now heads to Gov. Ned Lamont's desk.

"This bill makes targeted, meaningful investments in the people who keep our communities strong from first responders and veterans to nurses, teachers, and blue-collar workers," said Rep. Emmanuel "Manny" Sanchez, a Democrat from New Britain and committee co-chair, in a statement on his website.

The legislative session ends Wednesday, May 6.

value.Title: Truncated offshore wind study yields crucial community data

urn:contentItem:6JJV-SJ93-SG8H-M2DK-00000-00Truncated offshore wind study yields crucial community data2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSTATE AND REGIONAL NEWSTruncated offshore wind study yields crucial community dataJohn PenneyThe Day, New London, Conn.

New London — In 2024, a team of social scientists embarked on an ambitious plan to measure and track the effects of offshore wind projects on communities such as New London that serve as local hubs for the alternative energy ventures.

But in October, just 18 months into the work, federal grant funding for the multi-year project was yanked, effectively ending the planned assessment work, said David Bidwell, a professor of marine affairs at the University of Rhode Island, whose work focuses on the interplay between renewable energies technology and residents.

Despite the setback, the project was left with a massive amount of data gleaned through months of literature reviews, surveys and interviews with residents, labor members and others living in or near a port community used for offshore wind work.

On Tuesday, Bidwell, along with Scott Schnur, a senior fellow at URI, presented the group’s findings inside the New London Community Recreation Center at a forum hosted by the New London NAACP.

Bidwell said too often studies of offshore wind projects focused on “sticks in the water,” or the massive turbines that are the most visible aspect of such initiatives.

“But there’s also onshore impacts,” Bidwell said, noting his group looked to take a “bottom-up” approach to the issue that focused on concerns directly raised by residents at family, neighborhood and city levels.

“People were optimistic about potential benefits of these projects,” Bidwell said. “But there was also a fair amount of skepticism.”

Bidwell’s presentation was based on the results of interviews and community workshops held last year in New London; New Bedford, Mass.; and Turner Station, Md. The presentation included an overview of offshore wind energy development in the northeast U.S., as well as details on New London's role in the industry.

Bidwell said several common themes of concern were found, ranging from communication about a project and job creation to community impact and the environment.

“What people wanted most was more information,” Schnur said, adding many interviewees said offshore wind projects in their towns came “pre-made,” or essentially green-lit before they had a chance to add their opinions.

He said jobs while jobs were an important project aspect, residents wanted to ensure those positions were stable and available to them, and led to advancement.

Two projects staged in New London

State Pier is serving as the staging area for two major offshore wind projects being built in federal waters: Revolution Wind and Sunrise Wind, both developed by the Danish company Ørsted in partnerships with other firms. Both projects suffered delays due to stop-work orders — later overturned by federal courts — directed by President Donald Trump, a vocal critic of offshore wind work.

Sixty of the 64 turbines that will make up the 704-megawatt Revolution Wind project have been installed, while six of the 84 turbines set to be installed for the 924-megawatt Sunrise Wind project are in place, Paul Whitescarver, chairman of the Connecticut Port Authority’s Board of Directors, said Tuesday.

The Revolution Wind site is located about 15 miles south of the Rhode Island coast and began delivering power to the New England grid in March. Both Rhode Island and Connecticut have 20-year project power purchase agreements — Connecticut for a 304-megawatt share of the power — and Revolution Wind is expected to power 350,000 homes.

Work on Sunrise Wind, which will deliver enough energy to power 600,000 New York homes, continues roughly 19 miles south of Martha’s Vineyard. The wind farm is expected to be fully operational by 2027.

ISO New England, the nonprofit organization that runs the New England electric grid, reported that as of March, wind energy accounted for 7% of New England’s electricity.

Concerns and hopes

The concept of “broken promises” was also raised by study participants who worried that rosy assurances by wind developers and others might fall short.

Schnur said some New London residents referenced the Fort Trumbull debacle of the 1990s, in which a neighborhood was cleared by eminent domain for economic development purposes but is just now beginning to be redeveloped.

Other residents were hopeful that a wind project would put their cities and towns “back on the map,” Schnur said, adding that the issue of climate change was “surprisingly” not a major concern.

Bidwell said he’d hoped to use the collected data to develop a system to track participants’ concerns over time, using business tax revenue, wage and air quality data.

But with no federal grant money to use, the group is instead exploring ways to continue its tracking work at the local level, though there are no new funding sources yet identified.

Bidwell said attorneys general from some states have filed lawsuits attempting to get the federal dollars flowing again.

“It’s a slim possibility, but I’m not holding my breath,” he said.

j.penney@theday.com

value.Title: Connecticut Technical Education and Career System Celebrates the Completion of Seven Solar Energy Projects Across the State

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGJ-3333-SBBY-F0DB-00000-00Connecticut Technical Education and Career System Celebrates the Completion of Seven Solar Energy Projects Across the State2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:42ZLexisNexisConnecticut Technical Education and Career System Celebrates the Completion of Seven Solar Energy Projects Across the State

Key Highlights:

* Projects will save CTECS approximately $5.4 million in energy costs over lifetime.

* Solar energy systems reduce carbon emissions by 2,290 metric tons annually.

* Projects showcase commitment to sustainability and hands-on learning opportunities for students.

Original Press Release:

May 1 -- CT Green Bank issued the following news release:

- Seven projects to provide nearly 4 megawatts of clean energy and deliver approximately $5.4 million in lifetime energy cost savings

The Connecticut Technical Education and Career System (CTECS) and Connecticut Department of Administrative Services (DAS), along with partners the Connecticut Green Bank (Green Bank) and Verogy, today announced that seven solar energy projects at technical high schools across the state are nearing final completion.

The seven projects, including rooftop and carport installations, will collectively deliver almost 4 megawatts of clean, renewable energy to the schools. Additionally, CTECS will save approximately $5.4 million in energy costs over the lifetime of the panels while reducing carbon emissions by the equivalent of approximately 2,290 metric tons annually.

CTECS Executive Director Dr. Alice Pritchard, DAS Commissioner Michelle Gilman, Connecticut Green Bank Executive Vice President of Financing Programs Mackey Dykes, and Verogy Chief Operating Officer Steven DeNino were joined by students, state and local officials, and other project partners at a ceremonial ribbon-cutting event for a 756.8-kilowatt (kW) carport system serving Howell Cheney Technical High School in Manchester, one of the schools benefiting from solar.

"Today's ribbon cutting marks more than the completion of a project, it reflects a commitment to using our resources more efficiently to benefit students while also exposing them to emerging energy technologies," said CTECS Executive Director Dr. Alice Pritchard. "We're especially grateful to our project partners for their collaboration in bringing these projects to completion so quickly."

"DAS is proud to be part of a project that reflects Connecticut's strong commitment to sustainability," said DAS Commissioner Michelle Gilman. "By expanding clean energy at CTECS campuses, we're reducing costs, lowering emissions, and creating hands-on learning opportunities that prepare students for careers in a growing green economy."

"These projects are the result of significant coordination among CTECS, DAS, Verogy, and the Green Bank," said Mackey Dykes, Executive Vice President of Financing Programs at the Connecticut Green Bank. "Using Solar MAP+, we have helped streamline the process of going solar for state and municipal properties and affordable multifamily housing. We are excited to see these CTECS projects start producing savings."

"It's a privilege to help deliver clean, renewable energy to Connecticut's technical schools, and we're proud of what this team accomplished together," said Steve DeNino, Chief Operating Officer, Verogy. "As a Connecticut-based company, these projects hit close to home. Having the chance to give CTECS students a firsthand look at where the solar industry is headed made these projects especially rewarding."

"This ribbon-cutting ceremony represents more than just the installation of solar panels; it represents a commitment to our future, fellow students, and our community," said Olivia Garreffa, senior Electrical student at Howell Cheney Technical High School.

In addition to the carport system serving Howell Cheney Technical High School, the other schools benefiting from the solar energy systems include:

* W.F. Kaynor Technical High School (Waterbury) - 448.8 kW rooftop system

*A.I. Prince Technical High School (Hartford) - 170.5 kW rooftop system

*Eli Whitney Technical High School (Hamden) - 832.7 kW rooftop and carport systems

*Emmett O'Brien Technical High School (Ansonia) - 897.6 kW rooftop and carport systems

*Henry Abbott Technical High School (Danbury) - 656.7 kW carport system

*H.H. Ellis Technical High School (Danielson) - 959.2 kW roof and carport system

Full speaking program: Cheney Tech Solar Ribbon Cutting and Dedication(https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=47GIPisnx18)

About the Connecticut Technical Education and Career System

Through exemplary trade and academic programming, the Connecticut Technical Education and Career System (CTECS) prepares high school students and adult learners to meet the skilled workforce needs of Connecticut employers. CTECS operates 17 diploma-granting technical high schools, one technical education center for grades 11 and 12 and adult learners, and two aviation maintenance technician training programs. CTECS serves approximately 11,500 full-time high school students in over 30 Career and Technical Education (CTE) programs. In addition, over 3,000 adult learners take part in day courses, and evening apprenticeship and extension courses. All CTECS academic and CTE curriculum are designed with relevant, 21st-century employability skills required by business and industry.

About the Connecticut Department of Administrative Services

The Connecticut Department of Administrative Services (DAS) provides timely and cost-effective services to state agencies, state employees, municipalities, private industry, and the public. DAS offers a diverse array of programs, encompassing statewide human resources, information technology, fleet vehicle services, building and construction, procurement, grants administration, and beyond.

About the Connecticut Green Bank

The Connecticut Green Bank was established by the Connecticut General Assembly in 2011 as the nation's first state-level green bank and has since supported the creation of more than 30,000 green jobs in the state, while reducing the energy cost burden on over 71,000 families, businesses, and nonprofits. The Green Bank's vision is a planet protected by the love of humanity and its mission is to confront climate change by increasing and accelerating investment into Connecticut's green economy to create more resilient, healthier, and equitable communities. This is accomplished by leveraging limited public resources to scale-up and mobilize private capital investment into Connecticut. In 2017, the Connecticut Green Bank received the Innovations in American Government Award from the Harvard Kennedy School Ash Center for Democratic Governance and Innovation for their "Sparking the Green Bank Movement" entry. To date, the Green Bank has mobilized more than $3 billion into the State's green economy. This has reduced the energy costs for thousands of families and businesses, and reduced greenhouse gas emissions that cause climate change and worsen public health. In 2021, the Green Bank's model was expanded to include new areas of environmental infrastructure, related to climate adaptation and resiliency, land conservation, parks and recreation, agriculture, water, waste and recycling, and environmental markets, including carbon offsets and ecosystem services.

For more information about the Connecticut Green Bank, please visit www.ctgreenbank.com.

About Verogy

West Hartford, Connecticut-based Verogy is a leading solar developer with unmatched experience advancing customized commercial, industrial, and utility-scale renewable energy projects. With a diversified portfolio of cutting-edge renewable energy projects and a robust, growing pipeline, Verogy is actively delivering solutions across more than 20 states. Since its founding, Verogy has supported projects in the production of over 200,000 MWH of renewable energy. From project financing to design and construction, Verogy delivers renewable energy solutions that help communities, businesses, and institutions achieve their sustainability goals. Learn more at https://www.verogy.com.

[Category: Renewable Energy, Energy, Education, Sustainability Initiatives, ESG and Sustainability]

value.Title: STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-F693-RS9D-Y4SP-00000-00STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSTOCKSTONE POINT CAPITAL LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC

PermID4297094217Websitehttps://www.stonepoint.com/private-equity/IndustryPrivate EquityAddress20 Horseneck Lane Greenwich, CT 06830

ACTIVITIES:

Stone Point Capital LLC, founded in 2005, is a Greenwich, Connecticut-based private equity firm managing $45 billion in assets. Specializing in financial services, it invests in insurance, asset management, and fintech companies. With 70 professionals, Stone Point targets investments between $50 million and $1 billion. Notable portfolio companies include Alliant Insurance and Focus Financial Partners. The firms industry expertise and operational support drive portfolio growth. Stone Points disciplined investment strategy and strong industry relationships ensure consistent value creation.

+ STONE POINT CAPITAL LLC [1 News That Matters]

+ VENSURE NETS $450 MILLION DELAYED DRAW FACILITY TO DRIVE GROWTH AND M&A

Vensure Employer Solutions has secured $450 million from Stone Point Capital Markets to back its M&A strategy, via a Delayed Draw Term Loan upsized from $300 million. The financing was announced on the 23rd of March 2026.The funds will support continued growth and more acquisitions, with CEO Alex Campos acknowledging Stone Point's ongoing support.Since 2025, Vensure has integrated over 100 companies, including 76 post-2021 deals. The company serves more than 161,000 clients and processes more than $150 billion in payroll annually.Recent technology acquisitions, such as Distro and CreAI, broaden Vensure's automation and data intelligence capabilities, with further transactions anticipated this year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

value.Title: Interstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Years

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JMB-3723-RS7Y-J01N-00000-00Interstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Years2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisInterstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Yearsby Sam Weisberg

Interstate Waste Services Inc., backed by Littlejohn & Co. LLC and the private equity unit of Ares Management Corp., foresees a potential exit window of 24 to 36 months, said executive chairman Brian D'Amico.The Teaneck, N.J.-based business, with slightly more than $1 billion in annual revenue, doesn't anticipate a sale happening before most or all of the New York City zones it operates in are fully implemented into the NYC Department of Sanitation's Commercial Waste Zones, or CWZ, program, he explained.Via the program, IWS, which provides solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal services, has been awarded to be an authorized waste carter within 15 zones citywide, through its subsidiary Action Environmental Services Inc. Four of these zones have "gone live" - or are now complying with the program's mandates to reduce waste truck traffic and emissions and offer more efficient and safer recycling and organic waste treatment, among other functions, D'Amico noted on the sidelines of the ONE Houlihan Lokey Global Conference on Tuesday.Ideally, IWS will be close to realizing its CWZ goals by the time it exits, said D'Amico, though he added that a deal could happen sooner depending on market conditions.For the time being, one of its key goals is to keep executing on "mining the waste streams we collect every day and creating renewable gases," he said.IWS's services include converting landfill methanes and food waste into renewable energy.He acknowledged that many large infrastructure investors could be interested in buying IWS as "there are not a lot of platforms of our size."Ares and Littlejohn declined to be interviewed for this article.Meanwhile, IWS is actively seeking acquisitions, with "a handful of deals" signed, said D'Amico.

It is looking at bolt-on targets with $50 million to $100 million in annual revenue as well as tuck-ins with $20 million or less in revenue, to build density in existing markets. Targets could include hauling businesses with or without transfer stations, he added.Action has made 11 recent acquisitions in NYC, D'Amico said, adding that it will keep buying companies based in the city to maintain its strong standing in the CWZ program. NYC businesses that failed to win contracts in their zones are "looking to sell, and we're taking advantage of that," D'Amico noted.However, one of the waste management groups it bought - Filco Carting Corp., in December - had been awarded three zones in Manhattan.Last month, Action was named a designated hauler for the Midtown South Commercial Waste Zone, ensuring that new as well as existing customers in the zone, including the Ritz-Carlton Hotel, will continue to use Action as a provider.Outside of NYC, IWS operates in Connecticut, Central and Northern New Jersey, and N.Y.'s Westchester and Hudson Valley regions. IWS wants to expand into the Southern New Jersey and Philadelphia markets, as well as Northern and Eastern Connecticut, as it primarily services other regions of that state, said the chairman. It could achieve this goal via acquisitions, he added.IWS doesn't use an outside financial adviser for add-ons. Its law firm is Kirkland & Ellis LLP, said D'Amico.In early 2020, Action, then owned by Summer Street Capital Partners, LLC - where D'Amico is still a managing partner - became part of Summer Street-backed IWS via its merger with Prophet Equity-backed Apex Environmental. Littlejohn led the equity investment in IWS, with Summer Street, Prophet, and IWS management retaining significant stakes. Co-investor O-Corp Investments, LLC, also took a position in IWS.In October 2023, Ares' funds became co-owners of IWS with Littlejohn. IWS also completed a debt refinancing with a JP Morgan-led arranger group. Summer Street no longer holds a position in IWS, although Prophet and O-Corp remain as shareholders, according to D'Amico.Brown Gibbons Lang & Co. and Houlihan Lokey Inc. were financial advisers to IWS on the 2023 recap. The company used Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP as legal counsel. Robert W. Baird & Co. was exclusive financial adviser and Kirkland & Ellis LLP was legal counsel to Ares.

DEAL SIZE

$ 1-10 Billion

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-2457-00000-00May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 20252026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects.

The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support.

Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions.

The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

INDEX

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

SECTION 2 YALE UNIVERSITY TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Yale University, founded in 1701, is an Ivy League institution in New Haven, Connecticut. With around 15,000 employees and 12,000 students, it generates approximately $4.5 billion in annual revenue. Yale excels in humanities, sciences, and professional schools like law and medicine, with notable alumni including five U.S. presidents. In 2024, it expanded sustainability research. Competing with Harvard and Princeton, Yales mission is to educate leaders and advance discovery through rigorous scholarship and global outreach.

Mission

Yale's mission is to improve the world through exceptional research, education, and community engagement. By fostering a diverse, ethical, and collaborative environment, the university educates aspiring leaders dedicated to serving society across all sectors.

Vision

Yale aims to be the most student-focused research university globally. This vision is realized through faculty dedication to teaching, close engagement with students, and ensuring that all students benefit from Yale's extraordinary resources and programs.

Core Values

Excellence: Commitment to the highest standards in all endeavors.

Integrity: Ethical principles guide every decision and action.

Diversity and Inclusion: A welcoming environment that celebrates diverse perspectives and backgrounds.

Community: A collaborative culture that supports mutual growth and respect.

Service: Empowering members to positively impact society.

Research and Innovation

As a leader in research, Yale drives discovery across disciplines, preserving cultural and scientific heritage while inspiring innovation for future generations.

From its storied past to its ongoing contributions to society, Yale University continues to illuminate the world with its commitment to Lux et Veritas.

value.Title: As Clean Energy Jobs Grow, CT State Prepares Students For Workforce Demand

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-G323-RWFS-31X3-00000-00As Clean Energy Jobs Grow, CT State Prepares Students For Workforce Demand2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisEDUCATIONPg. 7BAs Clean Energy Jobs Grow, CT State Prepares Students For Workforce Demand

More than 47,000 jobs are supported by Connecticut’s clean energy sector, which continues to outpace overall state employment growth, according to the 2025 Connecticut Clean Energy Industry Report. Connecticut State Community College (CT State) is training students to meet that workforce demand through programs focused on energy, environmental, and sustainability careers.

One example is the Energy Management program at CT State Tunxis, which prepares students for roles such as energy auditor and energy analyst. Students learn to evaluate energy use, analyze building systems, and identify efficiency improvements across commercial, industrial, municipal, and utility settings, with hands-on experience in HVAC systems, lighting technologies, industrial processes, and renewable energy systems.

Michelle Coach, EdD, school dean of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) at CT State, said employers across Connecticut need people who can step into roles in energy, environmental, and sustainability fields right away.

“Energy efficiency is a major driver of that demand, and our programs give students the practical skills to move directly into the workforce or continue their education," Coach added.

CT State offers programs aligned to these workforce needs across clean energy, environmental science, and sustainability fields, including:

*Clean Water Management (certificate) *Environmental Engineering Technology (degrees and certificates) *Environmental Science (degrees and certificates, with a Sustainability option) *Horticulture (degree and certificates) *Technology Studies (including Energy Management and Environmental Science options)

Some programs include stackable credentials and transfer pathways, including an option for Energy Management students to transfer directly to Central Connecticut State University. Through grant funding from the Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection, CT State offers scholarships that may cover the full cost of the Energy Management program for eligible students who do not qualify for the college’s free tuition program.

Graduates are prepared for careers in environmental protection, engineering technology, water resource management, and sustainability planning fields that support infrastructure, natural resources, and economic growth across Connecticut.

Students interested in CT State’s environmental and energy programs can learn more at ctstate.edu. Credit registration is in progress at CT State campuses statewide. Fall 2026 semester credit classes begin on Thursday, August 27 with a mix of on-campus, online, and hybrid course options.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SPRINGFIELD, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Eversource Energy has shown some strength in recent days. Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES), the NYSE's 12th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, climbed 2.2% during its previous trading session to close at $68.73.

Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) is a leading utility company specializing in delivering electric and natural gas services across the Northeast. Its core business focuses on clean energy infrastructure, underpinned by significant investments in renewable energy projects and grid modernization such as smart grid solutions and transmission line construction. Eversource's innovation pipeline includes initiatives like battery energy storage and community solar, highlighting its commitment to sustainable energy solutions. Strategically, Eversource is positioned to enhance North America's clean infrastructure through continued advancements in power reliability and sustainable investments. This focus, combined with robust energy efficiency programs, underscores Eversource's competitive edge in the utility landscape.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 276 words

Wed May 6, 2026: Eversource Energy Launches Main Streets Program to Support Local Businesses

MIDDLETOWN, Conn. — Eversource Energy is taking significant steps to assist small businesses in Connecticut by helping them reduce their energy usage and associated costs. This initiative, part of the company's long-standing commitment to enhancing energy efficiency, was officially announced on the 6th of May 2026.

The Main Streets Program, which has garnered awards for its effectiveness, will expand its reach to eight cities and towns throughout the year. The program commences with a comprehensive assessment carried out by certified specialists who identify various energy-saving opportunities. Upgrades may include weatherization measures, insulation enhancements, and the installation of programmable thermostats.

Recently, crews were seen at the St. Francis of Assisi church in Middletown, where they identified several potential upgrades. Ricardo Jordan, the manager of energy efficiency at Eversource Energy, noted, "So far, we've definitely seen some lighting opportunities. The church has some old fluorescent technology, and we can replace that with LEDs with controls, and we've also identified some insulation and weather stripping opportunities in the basement areas. Typically, it can result in hundreds of dollars of savings over the course of the year."

In the coming week, Eversource Energy's crews are scheduled to visit New Britain to continue their work under the Main Streets initiative. Business owners interested in learning more about the program, including event schedules and how to book an assessment for their establishments, are encouraged to seek further information through the designated channels.

Eversource Energy's commitment to empowering small businesses through energy efficiency not only helps reduce operational costs but also contributes to a more sustainable future. As the program progresses, it promises to play a crucial role in supporting Connecticut's local economy.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 362 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Eversource Energy and Agri-Mark Collaborate on Innovative Energy Solutions

Eversource Energy is partnering with Agri-Mark to implement energy-efficient upgrades aimed at enhancing the operational efficiency of dairy production. This collaboration involves the installation of a high-efficiency mechanical vapor recompression (MVR) unit, specifically designed for the production of condensed and powdered milk products. The announcement follows the completion of the installation on the 5th of May, 2026, marking a significant milestone in reducing energy consumption within the agricultural sector.

The new MVR unit replaces Agri-Mark's former steam-based thermal vapor recompression (TVR) system, which had been increasingly reliant on a natural gas-fired boiler. This transition is expected to significantly lower energy usage while enhancing product quality and operational consistency. Eversource Energy's support, through incentives from the Mass Save® program, has been pivotal in helping Agri-Mark identify and implement these energy-saving solutions.

With the upgraded MVR system, Agri-Mark anticipates a reduction in natural gas consumption by an estimated 967,000 therms annually. This represents not only a decrease in overall energy usage but also contributes to the environmental sustainability of the cooperative, which is owned by hundreds of dairy farm families across New England and New York. In addition to the MVR upgrade, Eversource Energy has also facilitated the installation of a new energy-efficient Finisher TVR system, which is expected to further reduce natural gas consumption by an additional 110,000 therms per year.

The operational benefits of this partnership are already becoming apparent. The new systems have resulted in more consistent product temperatures, reduced maintenance needs, and improved overall reliability. These enhancements are crucial for a high-volume plant that operates continuously to meet the demands of regional dairy farmers. Penni McLean-Conner, Eversource's Executive Vice President of Customer Experience and Energy Strategy, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating that it exemplifies practical innovation that keeps essential manufacturing operations efficient and affordable.

Jed Davis, Vice President of Strategic Engagement & Sustainability at Agri-Mark, expressed optimism about the future, underscoring the cooperative's commitment to sustainable operations. He noted that this upgrade not only lowers their environmental footprint but also supports the long-term viability of the cooperative. The partnership with Eversource Energy is expected to enhance Agri-Mark's ability to produce award-winning dairy products while maintaining responsible operational practices.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 265 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Eversource's $315 Million Project Set to Disrupt Hartford's West End

HARTFORD, Conn. (WFSB) - Hartford's West End is bracing for a summer of road closures, detours, and lane closures as part of a $315 million underground project to upgrade the city's electric grid. The bulk of the work in the neighborhood starts on the 4th of May 2026 and will last two to three months.

Eversource Energy, which is spearheading the project, is replacing 52-year-old transmission lines with more advanced cables designed to enhance the electric grid. The majority of the work will take place underground, but drivers can expect visible signs of construction, including road closures and detours that will reroute traffic through quieter neighborhood streets.

Local resident Janet Morrison, who has lived in the West End for nine years, acknowledged the necessity of the Eversource project but expressed concerns about the inconveniences it will bring. 'At rush hour, you'll see more traffic through the neighborhood on what are normally quieter streets due to the detours that have been set up,' Morrison stated.

The construction is expected to affect an already congested stretch of Asylum Avenue at Scarborough and Whitney streets. An Eversource spokesperson stated that the project 'will enhance the safety and reliability of the electric system and increase its capacity to meet current and future energy demand in the Hartford area.'

This significant upgrade involves replacing nearly seven miles of transmission lines. Eversource has been engaging with residents since 2023 to discuss the project's impact and is committed to minimizing disruptions during the construction period. The massive underground initiative, which began last April, is anticipated to conclude in the middle of 2027.

5.4 Created Market Value [CMV] past 18 yrs of $17.4 billion

This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $3.6 billion and Created Market Value of $17.4 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $5.89.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Eversource Energy, a leading utility company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), operates primarily in the northeastern United States, delivering reliable electric power and natural gas services to millions of customers. The company's core activities encompass electric transmission and distribution, natural gas delivery, and water services, with a strong emphasis on infrastructure maintenance and energy efficiency initiatives. In recent years, Eversource has experienced rapid growth in its renewable energy segments, particularly offshore wind projects and clean energy solutions, driven by increasing demand for sustainable power sources and supportive regulatory environments. These expansions, including investments in large-scale wind farms and electric vehicle charging infrastructure, have positioned the company as a key player in the transition to greener energy, while enhancing its competitive edge and long-term revenue potential. Overall, Eversource's strategic focus on innovation and sustainability supports its commitment to operational excellence and community service. It is the NYSE's 12th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) stands as a leading utility in the northeastern United States, leveraging its extensive Eversource vegetation management program to maintain high service reliability and environmental stewardship. Integral to Eversource right-of-way maintenance, the company focuses on Eversource tree trimming and Eversource hazardous tree removal, which are pivotal to its outage prevention strategies. Key elements such as Eversource power line tree trimming and Eversource vegetation inspections underscore its commitment to effective utility vegetation management. With a robust Eversource trimming schedule, Eversource assures its stakeholders of systematic Eversource line clearance, bolstering grid reliability. Eversource trimming schedule aligns with the industry's vegetation management best practices, reinforcing Eversource vegetation safety standards. These practices contribute to significant storm hardening through Eversource storm hardening vegetation initiatives. Offering Eversource arborist services and partnering with vegetation management contractors, Eversource excels in roadside vegetation control, positioning itself as a strategic leader in the electric utility vegetation management program.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Eversource's $315 Million Project Set to Disrupt Hartford's West End

HARTFORD, Conn. (WFSB) - Hartford's West End is bracing for a summer of road closures, detours, and lane closures as part of a $315 million underground project to upgrade the city's electric grid. The bulk of the work in the neighborhood starts on the 4th of May 2026 and will last two to three months.

Eversource Energy, which is spearheading the project, is replacing 52-year-old transmission lines with more advanced cables designed to enhance the electric grid. The majority of the work will take place underground, but drivers can expect visible signs of construction, including road closures and detours that will reroute traffic through quieter neighborhood streets.

Local resident Janet Morrison, who has lived in the West End for nine years, acknowledged the necessity of the Eversource project but expressed concerns about the inconveniences it will bring. 'At rush hour, you'll see more traffic through the neighborhood on what are normally quieter streets due to the detours that have been set up,' Morrison stated.

The construction is expected to affect an already congested stretch of Asylum Avenue at Scarborough and Whitney streets. An Eversource spokesperson stated that the project 'will enhance the safety and reliability of the electric system and increase its capacity to meet current and future energy demand in the Hartford area.'

This significant upgrade involves replacing nearly seven miles of transmission lines. Eversource has been engaging with residents since 2023 to discuss the project's impact and is committed to minimizing disruptions during the construction period. The massive underground initiative, which began last April, is anticipated to conclude in the middle of 2027.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Eversource Energy, a leading utility company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), operates primarily in the northeastern United States, delivering reliable electric power and natural gas services to millions of customers. The company's core activities encompass electric transmission and distribution, natural gas delivery, and water services, with a strong emphasis on infrastructure maintenance and energy efficiency initiatives. In recent years, Eversource has experienced rapid growth in its renewable energy segments, particularly offshore wind projects and clean energy solutions, driven by increasing demand for sustainable power sources and supportive regulatory environments. These expansions, including investments in large-scale wind farms and electric vehicle charging infrastructure, have positioned the company as a key player in the transition to greener energy, while enhancing its competitive edge and long-term revenue potential. Overall, Eversource's strategic focus on innovation and sustainability supports its commitment to operational excellence and community service. It is the NYSE's 12th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) stands as a leading utility in the northeastern United States, leveraging its extensive Eversource vegetation management program to maintain high service reliability and environmental stewardship. Integral to Eversource right-of-way maintenance, the company focuses on Eversource tree trimming and Eversource hazardous tree removal, which are pivotal to its outage prevention strategies. Key elements such as Eversource power line tree trimming and Eversource vegetation inspections underscore its commitment to effective utility vegetation management. With a robust Eversource trimming schedule, Eversource assures its stakeholders of systematic Eversource line clearance, bolstering grid reliability. Eversource trimming schedule aligns with the industry's vegetation management best practices, reinforcing Eversource vegetation safety standards. These practices contribute to significant storm hardening through Eversource storm hardening vegetation initiatives. Offering Eversource arborist services and partnering with vegetation management contractors, Eversource excels in roadside vegetation control, positioning itself as a strategic leader in the electric utility vegetation management program.
